message:
fix(prompt): improve line range parsing for Windows paths and validation

Harden ParseFileSpec() to correctly handle Windows drive letters and
add stricter validation for line range specifications.

Windows path support:
- Add isLineRangeSuffix() helper that strictly validates what follows
  a colon: must contain only digits and at most one hyphen
- "C:\foo\bar.go" now correctly parses as path="C:\foo\bar.go" instead
  of mis-parsing the drive letter as a line range
- Rejects ambiguous suffixes like lone "-" or non-numeric content

Validation improvements:
- Reject line numbers < 1 (must be positive)
- Reject inverted ranges where start > end (e.g., "file:50-10")
- Return explicit errors instead of silently falling back to treating
  the whole spec as a path

MaxFileSize constant:
- Add MaxFileSize = 1MB constant for context injection limits
- Documents the safety limit for file content injection

New isLineRangeSuffix() function:
- Validates suffix contains only digits (at least one) and optionally
  a single hyphen
- Ensures hyphen isn't the only character
- Used to distinguish "path:10-50" from "path:with:colons"

Test additions:
- Add test cases for Windows paths (C:\foo\bar.go)
- Add test cases for validation errors

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -21,6 +21,11 @@ func TestParseFileSpec(t *testing.T) {
 		{"file.go:-50", "file.go", 0, 50},
 		{"file.go:25", "file.go", 25, 25},
 		{"/abs/path/file.go:1-10", "/abs/path/file.go", 1, 10},
+		{"file.go:abc-1", "file.go:abc-1", 0, 0},                 // not a line range suffix
+		{`C:\proj-1\main.go`, `C:\proj-1\main.go`, 0, 0},         // Windows drive path (no range)
+		{`C:\proj-1\main.go:12-34`, `C:\proj-1\main.go`, 12, 34}, // Windows drive path with range
+		{`C:\proj-1\main.go:12-`, `C:\proj-1\main.go`, 12, 0},    // Windows drive path with open range
+		{`C:\proj-1\main.go:-34`, `C:\proj-1\main.go`, 0, 34},    // Windows drive path with end-only range
 	}
 
 	for _, tt := range tests {
